steps.title: Map the two texts
steps.content: Text 1 reports Lin and colleagues' mammoth eDNA finding: mammoth DNA appeared in sediment layers much later than the fossil record would predict. It then gives Price and Ellison's explanation that preserved surface carcasses could have leaked DNA for thousands of years.

steps.title: Describe the challenge
steps.content: Text 2 adds a problem for that explanation: surface remains would probably be broken down by scavengers and weathering before a thousand years passed. A invents two research approaches. C wrongly says Text 1 critiques Lin's methods and Text 2 defends them. D reverses the relationship, since Text 2 challenges rather than reconciles the explanation from Text 1. The answer is B.
